children.name: Pogona vitticeps
children.description: The most popular pet lizard globally; inflates and darkens beard as a threat display; sex determined partly by temperature — genetically male individuals hatch as functional females in hot conditions.

children.name: Pogona barbata
children.description: More arboreal; impressive beard displays; eastern Australia.

children.name: Agama
children.description: Common agamas of Africa and Eurasia.

children.name: Agama agama
children.description: Male's head and anterior turn vivid orange-red when basking in sunlight and reverts to grey when cool or submissive; highly territorial; pushup displays.

children.name: Agama stellio
children.description: Eastern Mediterranean; basking on ancient walls and ruins; tourist attraction in Greece.

children.name: Draco volans
children.description: Glides between trees on elongated rib-supported membranes; covers over 9 m per glide; males display vivid red, yellow, and blue dewlaps; returns to home tree to roost.

children.name: Draco blanfordii
children.description: Similar gliding lizard; mainland SE Asia.

children.name: Moloch
children.description: The thorny devil of Australian desert.

children.name: Moloch horridus
children.description: Entire body covered in recurved protective spines; hygroscopic channels between scales collect dew and rainwater, delivering it directly to the mouth; eats only ants — up to 2,500 per meal.

children.name: Hydrosaurus amboinensis
children.description: Spectacular dorsal sail; runs bipedally at high speed across water surfaces; eats leaves and fruit.

children.name: Physignathus cocincinus
children.description: Popular in captivity; vivid green; powerful swimmer; can remain submerged for 25 minutes.
